beats: ONE single loopy long-fingered hand, open palm up, holding out one plain finished cream object, with a small closed cream toolbox sitting unopened beside it. Exactly one hand in the whole picture. That hand must show FIVE digits: four finger loops and one separate thumb. Drawn flat and straight on with no depth.

one loopy long-fingered hand with exactly four fingers and one thumb turning a crank on a flat cream machine, with exactly two identical plain cream objects standing beside it, four drawn objects in total, drawn flat and straight on

a large flat grid of many small cream squares filling the lower half of the frame, seen straight on with no depth, held from beneath by one large open loopy long-fingered hand with four fingers and a thumb
two loopy long-fingered hands, the left one holding a small cream key, the right one resting flat on a large plain cream slab, the key clearly belonging to the hand and not to the slab
pageText: Nobody wants the tool. They want the thing the tool makes.
So stop selling the tool. Sell the machine that makes the outcome, again and again.
The hard part was never making one. It was making the ten thousandth one just as good.
Rent the slab. Keep the key. The key is the only part anyone can copy you for.
